title: Use random string for getting a unique container name
body:
Currently a buildah bud unique container name is found by incrementing
a counter and checking if that container exists. This causes a race
where that name may be claimed by another run by the time the
container is created.

This change replaces the incremented counter with a random string, so
that no duplicate name check is required.

Fixes #1004

Signed-off-by: Steve Baker <sbaker@redhat.com>

comments.body:
Can one of the admins verify this patch?
I understand the following commands:
- `bot, add author to whitelist`
- `bot, test pull request`
- `bot, test pull request once`

Can you add some test output to see what the new container name looks like?

I really like the older numbering system, I wonder if we could throw up a lock around the old code instead of doing a randomid.  @nalind WDYT?
@steveb Thanks for this PR.  @nalind Submitted a different version.  And we are going to use his.  Great find.
I'll test the fix now, thanks
